question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user set the source URI to a DNS-rebinding target that initially resolves to a legitimate S3 endpoint (passing any validation) but later resolves to an attacker-controlled server, causing signed requests to be sent to the attacker's server and authorizing transaction ingestion from an unapproved source?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that AnonymousCredentialsProvider.create() produces a singleton that is shared across all S3 clients built in the same JVM, causing a race condition where a legitimately authenticated client is replaced by an anonymous one, authorizing transactions without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the region string passed to Region.of(sourceProperties.getRegion()) to include a region that maps to a GovCloud or China partition endpoint where the importer's IAM policies do not apply, authorizing transaction downloads without the owner's cross-partition approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of source type validation in the switch statement (LOCAL, GCP, S3) to cause an unhandled case that defaults to a permissive provider, authorizing transaction stream ingestion without proper signer verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user set connectionMaxIdleTime below the AWS SDK minimum threshold, causing the NettyNioAsyncHttpClient to continuously create new connections that bypass connection-level authorization caching, authorizing each transaction request independently without owner-approved session reuse?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the static credential path by supplying an accessKey and secretKey that belong to a different AWS account, causing the importer to authorize transaction stream downloads from a bucket owned by a third party without that owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: overrideConfiguration()] Can an unprivileged external user set projectId to a value that causes the ExecutionInterceptor's modifyHttpRequest() to append a userProject parameter that overrides GCP's requester-pays authorization, causing transaction stream files to be billed and authorized under a victim project without its owner's consent?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of certificate pinning on the NettyNioAsyncHttpClient to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the S3 connection, intercepting and replacing stream files with forged transaction data that was never approved by legitimate signers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user cause blockProperties.getBucketName() to return an attacker-controlled bucket name, causing the S3StreamFileProvider to download block stream files from an unauthorized source, ingesting transactions that were never approved by the legitimate block signers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the credential provider selection logic to cause a GCP-typed source with a null credentials object to call AwsBasicCredentials.create(null, null), producing a credential object that AWS SDK accepts but that signs requests with empty keys, bypassing proper signer authorization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a URI with a path component (e.g., http://s3.amazonaws.com/attacker-bucket/) to endpointOverride, causing forcePathStyle(true) to construct S3 keys that resolve to the attacker's bucket, authorizing transaction downloads from an unapproved source?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the log.info() disclosure of bucket names and path prefixes to enumerate the storage topology, then craft requests to those buckets using anonymous access (if enabled) to authorize transaction stream downloads without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DefaultCredentialsProvider chain's ECS task metadata endpoint lookup to inject attacker-controlled credentials via a compromised ECS metadata service, causing the importer to authorize transaction stream downloads under an attacker identity without the owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that StaticCredentialsProvider caches credentials indefinitely (no rotation) to use a previously leaked accessKey/secretKey pair to authorize transaction stream downloads long after the legitimate owner has revoked those credentials?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NettyNioAsyncHttpClient's lack of proxy configuration to route S3 requests through an attacker-controlled HTTP proxy, intercepting AWS Signature V4 authorization headers and using them to authorize transaction downloads without the owner's knowledge?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of source count validation in the providers list to register an unbounded number of S3 sources, each with different credentials, causing the importer to authorize transaction downloads from multiple unauthorized buckets simultaneously without owner approval?
